original_hypothesis.title: LockingMultiRewards recover() Can Drain Undistributed Reward Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LockingMultiRewards.recover() only protects the staking token balance but has no protection for reward token balances. The owner can call recover(rewardToken, fullBalance) to drain all undistributed reward tokens. This includes rewards already accrued by stakers but not yet claimed, and rewards in the current distribution period.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The recover function checks `if (tokenAddress == stakingToken && tokenAmount > stakingToken.balanceOf(address(this)) - stakingTokenBalance)` but applies no restriction for reward tokens. The function comment warns about this but the code provides no guardrails.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LockingMultiRewards.s.sol shows staking deployed with addReward(arb) and ownership transferred to safe.ops

proof_of_signal.pattern: Only staking token balance is protected; reward tokens can be fully drained by owner

preconditions: Owner has access to the recover function
Reward tokens have been deposited via notifyRewardAmount

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No balance protection for reward tokens; only stakingToken checked
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Owner can drain rewards that stakers have already earned but not yet claimed
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should track and protect reward token balances similarly to stakingTokenBalance

analysis_questions: What reward tokens are configured in deployed instances?
What is the total undistributed reward balance at any time?
Could this be triggered accidentally or maliciously?
validation_requirements: Verify stakers can no longer claim expected rewards
Confirm no revert on recover for non-staking tokens

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised owner calls recover(rewardToken, rewardToken.balanceOf(contract)) to drain all reward tokens. Stakers who have earned rewards based on rewardPerToken can no longer claim them. The getRewards function would revert on safeTransfer due to insufficient balance.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete loss of undistributed and unclaimed reward tokens
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner would need to re-deposit reward tokens

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for recover() impact on reward token availability

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable by monitoring reward token balance of contract

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
